title: maranthes gabunensis
intro: Maranthes gabunensis, also known as African breadnut or gabun nut, is a tropical plant species native to West Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The seeds of the plant contain a compound called gabunin, which has been shown to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory infections. In traditional herbal preparations, the seeds of the Maranthes gabunensis plant are often ground into a powder and used to treat various ailments, or are made into a decoction by boiling the seeds in water.
